examples.text:
عُذْرُ الْمَلُومَيْنِ فِيكَ أَنَّهُمَا
 آسٍ جَبَانٌ وَمِبْضَعٌ بَطَلُ

senses.gloss: to be valiant, to be heroic
to be or become null, void, futile, worthless, pointless

examples.english: When the reason is known, the wonder is lost.

senses.gloss: to nullify, to invalidate, to render ineffective, to rescind, to repeal, to revoke

etymology.text:
From Proto-Semitic *baṭal-.

The verb form I is inherited from Proto-Semitic *baṭal-.

See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.
